sentences.chinese: 而去病出北地，遂深入，合騎侯失道，不相得。
sentences.literal: But Qubing marched out of Beidi, went deep in; Marquis of Heqi lost the way and they did not link up.
sentences.idiomatic: Meanwhile Qubing pushed out from Beidi deep into the steppe; Marquis of Heqi Gongsun Ao missed his route and never joined him.

sentences.chinese: 去病至祁連山，捕首虜甚多。
sentences.literal: Qubing reached the Qilian Mountains and captured very many heads and prisoners.
sentences.idiomatic: Qubing reached the Qilian range and took a very large toll of heads and captives.

sentences.chinese: 上曰：「票騎將軍涉鈞耆，濟居延，遂臻小月氏，攻祁連山，揚武乎鱳得，得單于單桓、酋涂王，及相國、都尉以眾降下者二千五百人，可謂能舍服知成而止矣。
sentences.literal: The emperor said, “The swift-cavalry general crossed Junqi, forded Juyan, reached the Lesser Yuezhi, attacked the Qilian Mountains, displayed arms at Lude, took the shanyu’s kings Danhuan and Qiutu, and chancellors and commandants with the masses who surrendered—two thousand five hundred persons; he may be called one who knows how to spare those who submit, perceives when success is won, and stops.”
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor declared, “The swift-cavalry general crossed Junqi, crossed Juyan, advanced to the Lesser Yuezhi, stormed the Qilian line, struck fear at Lude, and brought in the shanyu’s kings Danhuan and Qiutu, together with chancellors, commandants, and more than two thousand five hundred men of their following who yielded. Here is a commander who knows when to accept surrender, recognize a settled victory, and halt.”

sentences.chinese: 捷首虜三萬二百，獲五王，王母、單于閼氏、王子五十九人，相國、將軍、當戶、都尉六十三人，師大率減什三，益封去病五千四百戶。
sentences.literal: He took heads and prisoners 30,200, captured five kings, the queen mother, the shanyu’s chief wife, and fifty-nine princes, sixty-three chancellors, generals, household heads, and commandants; the army lost on the whole three tenths of its strength—increase Qubing’s fief by 5,400 households.
sentences.idiomatic: The butcher’s bill ran to thirty thousand two hundred heads and prisoners, five kings, a queen mother, a shanyu consort, fifty-nine princes, and sixty-three chancellors, generals, household heads, and commandants. Casualties ran to about three in ten—increase Huo Qubing’s fief by fifty-four hundred households.

sentences.chinese: 賜校尉從至小月氏者爵左庶長。
sentences.literal: He granted the rank of left chief of the people to colonels who had followed him to the Lesser Yuezhi.
sentences.idiomatic: Colonels who had accompanied him to the Lesser Yuezhi were raised to the rank of left chief of the people.

sentences.chinese: 鷹擊司馬破奴再從票騎將軍斬遫濮王，捕稽且王，右千騎將王、王母各一人，王子以下四十一人，捕虜三千三百三十人，前行捕虜千四百人，封破奴為從票侯。
sentences.literal: Eagle-strike major Ponu twice followed the swift-cavalry general, beheaded King Chipu, captured King Jiju, one king and one queen mother of the right thousand-cavalry general, forty-one persons from princes downward, captured 3,330 prisoners, and in the van took 1,400 prisoners—enfeoff Ponu as marquis attendant on the swift-cavalry.
sentences.idiomatic: Major Ponu of the eagle-strike corps twice rode with the swift-cavalry general: he slew King Chipu, seized King Jiju, took a king and queen mother of the right thousand-cavalry command, forty-one nobles below the rank of prince, thirty-three hundred thirty captives, and fourteen hundred more in the lead column—invest Ponu as marquis attendant on the swift-cavalry.

sentences.chinese: 校尉高不識從票騎將軍捕呼于耆王王子以下十一人，捕虜千七百六十八人，封不識為宜冠侯。
sentences.literal: Colonel Gao Bushi followed the swift-cavalry general, captured King Huyuqi and eleven persons from princes downward, captured 1,768 prisoners—enfeoff Bushi as marquis of Yiguan.
sentences.idiomatic: Colonel Gao Bushi marched with the swift-cavalry general, seized King Huyuqi and eleven princes and nobles beneath him, and seventeen hundred sixty-eight captives—invest Bushi as marquis of Yiguan.

sentences.chinese: 校尉僕多有功，封為煇渠侯。」
sentences.literal: Colonel Puyou had merit and was enfeoffed as marquis of Huiqu.”
sentences.idiomatic: Colonel Puyou also earned distinction and was enfeoffed as marquis of Huiqu.”

sentences.chinese: 合騎侯敖坐留不與票騎將軍會，當斬，贖為庶人。
sentences.literal: Marquis of Heqi Ao, sitting for delay and failing to join the swift-cavalry general, was liable for execution; he ransomed his life and became a commoner.
sentences.idiomatic: Marquis of Heqi Gongsun Ao was sentenced to death for loitering and missing his rendezvous with the swift-cavalry general; he commuted the sentence and was stripped to commoner status.

sentences.chinese: 諸宿將所將士馬兵亦不如去病，去病所將常選，然亦敢深入，常與壯騎先其大軍，軍亦有天幸，未嘗困絕也。
sentences.literal: The officers, mounts, and men led by the veteran generals were also not the equal of Qubing’s; those Qubing led were always picked, yet he also dared to go deep, constantly with picked horse riding ahead of the great army, and the army also had Heaven’s luck and never was cut off in extremity.
sentences.idiomatic: The veteran generals could not match the quality of his troops—Qubing habitually led an elite corps—yet he still drove deep, usually racing ahead of the main body with picked riders, and his columns enjoyed a kind of fortune that never left them trapped or destitute.

sentences.chinese: 然而諸宿將常留落不耦。
sentences.literal: Yet the veteran generals often lagged behind and did not pair with him.
sentences.idiomatic: The older commanders, by contrast, habitually fell behind and failed to keep pace with him.

sentences.chinese: 由此去病日以親貴，比大將軍。
sentences.literal: From this Qubing daily grew closer in favor and honor, comparable to the great general.
sentences.idiomatic: Henceforward Qubing rose day by day in imperial favor until he stood on a par with the great general.

sentences.chinese: 其後，單于怒渾邪王居西方數為漢所破，亡數萬人，以票騎之兵也，欲召誅渾邪王。
sentences.literal: Afterward the shanyu was angry that King Hunyex, stationed in the west, had several times been broken by Han and had lost several tens of thousands of men to the swift-cavalry’s troops, and wished to summon and execute King Hunyex.
sentences.idiomatic: Later the shanyu, furious that King Hunyex in the west had been repeatedly shattered by Han and had lost tens of thousands of men to the swift-cavalry columns, resolved to summon him and put him to death.

sentences.chinese: 渾邪王與休屠王等謀欲降漢，使人先要道邊。
sentences.literal: King Hunyex with King Xiutu and others plotted to surrender to Han and sent men first to signal the border along the road.
sentences.idiomatic: King Hunyex conspired with King Xiutu and others to defect to Han and dispatched envoys to clear the way with the frontier posts.

sentences.chinese: 是時大行李息將城河上，得渾邪王使，即馳傳以聞。
sentences.literal: At that time Grand Messenger Li Xi was walling the Yellow River and captured King Hunyex’s envoy, immediately relaying post-horses to report.
sentences.idiomatic: Grand messenger Li Xi was then fortifying the Yellow River line; he intercepted King Hunyex’s messenger and rushed the news to court by post-station relay.

sentences.chinese: 上恐其以詐降而襲邊，乃令去病將兵往迎之。
sentences.literal: The emperor feared they might feign surrender and raid the border, and ordered Qubing to lead troops to go welcome them.
sentences.idiomatic: Fearing a false capitulation meant to ambush the frontier, the emperor ordered Qubing to take an army forward to receive them.

sentences.chinese: 去病既度河，與渾邪眾相望。
sentences.literal: Qubing, having crossed the river, was within sight of Hunyex’s horde.
sentences.idiomatic: Once Qubing had crossed the river, he drew up in full view of King Hunyex’s host.

sentences.chinese: 渾邪裨王將見漢軍而多欲不降者，頗遁去。
sentences.literal: Hunyex lieutenant kings who were to lead the meeting with Han troops and among whom many did not wish to surrender for the most part fled.
sentences.idiomatic: Among the lieutenant kings slated to parley with the Han, many had second thoughts about surrender and slipped away in large numbers.

sentences.chinese: 去病乃馳入，得與渾邪王相見，斬其欲亡者八千人，遂獨遣渾邪王乘傳先詣行在所，盡將其眾度河，降者數萬人，號稱十萬。
sentences.literal: Qubing then galloped in, was able to meet King Hunyex face to face, beheaded eight thousand of those who wished to flee, then alone sent King Hunyex riding post-carriages first to the traveling encampment, led all his masses across the river—several tens of thousands who surrendered, claiming a hundred thousand.
sentences.idiomatic: Qubing spurred straight into the column, met King Hunyex in person, and cut down eight thousand would-be deserters. He then put the king on relay carriages to the emperor’s field headquarters, shepherded the entire following across the river—several tens of thousands of capitulators who boasted their number at a hundred thousand.

sentences.chinese: 既至長安，天子所以賞賜數十鉅萬。
sentences.literal: When they reached Chang’an, what the Son of Heaven granted in rewards ran to several hundred million cash.
sentences.idiomatic: At Chang’an the emperor’s gifts ran into the hundreds of millions.

sentences.chinese: 封渾邪王萬戶，為漯陰侯。
sentences.literal: He enfeoffed King Hunyex with ten thousand households as marquis of Luoyin.
sentences.idiomatic: He invested King Hunyex with ten thousand households as marquis of Luoyin.

sentences.chinese: 封其裨王呼毒尼為下摩侯，雁疪為煇渠侯，禽黎為河綦侯，大當戶調雖為常樂侯。
sentences.literal: He enfeoffed his lieutenant kings: Hudu’ni as marquis of Xiamo, Yanzhi as marquis of Huiqu, Qinli as marquis of Heqi, grand household head Diaosui as marquis of Changle.
sentences.idiomatic: His lieutenant kings received titles as well: Hudu’ni as marquis of Xiamo, Yanzhi as marquis of Huiqu, Qinli as marquis of Heqi, and grand household head Diaosui as marquis of Changle.

sentences.chinese: 於是上嘉去病之功，曰：「票騎將軍去病率師征匈奴，西域王渾邪王及厥眾萌咸奔於率，以軍糧接食，并將控弦萬有餘人，誅獟悍，捷首虜八千餘級，降異國之王三十二。
sentences.literal: Thereupon the emperor praised Qubing’s merit, saying, “Swift-cavalry general Qubing led the army to chastise the Xiongnu; King Hunyex of the Western Regions and his dependent people all came in submission at the head, were fed with army grain, and together brought more than ten thousand bowmen; he executed the fierce and bold, took more than eight thousand heads and prisoners, and reduced thirty-two kings of foreign states.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor then extolled Qubing: “The swift-cavalry general led the host against the Xiongnu; King Hunyex of the Western Regions and his followers submitted in a body, drew rations from our baggage train, and mustered more than ten thousand archers. He cut down the die-hards, counted more than eight thousand heads and captives, and brought thirty-two foreign kings to heel.”

sentences.chinese: 戰士不離傷，十萬之眾畢懷集服。
sentences.literal: The fighting men neither scattered nor suffered grave harm; the horde of a hundred thousand all submitted in gathering allegiance.
sentences.idiomatic: His men held formation with light losses, and the vast host that had marched in now submitted as one.

sentences.chinese: 仍興之勞，爰及河塞，庶幾亡患。
sentences.literal: The labor of this undertaking extended to the passes of the Yellow River, so that perhaps there would be no further peril.
sentences.idiomatic: The effort reached the river frontier, easing the threat along the northern line.

sentences.chinese: 以千七百戶益封票騎將軍。
sentences.literal: Increase the swift-cavalry general’s fief by 1,700 households.
sentences.idiomatic: Add seventeen hundred households to the swift-cavalry general’s fief.

sentences.chinese: 減隴西、北地、上郡戍卒之半，以寬天下繇役。」
sentences.literal: Halve the garrison troops of Longxi, Beidi, and Shang commanderies, so as to lighten corvée labor for the realm.”
sentences.idiomatic: Cut the standing garrisons of Longxi, Beidi, and Shang by half to spare the empire’s labor levies.”

sentences.chinese: 乃分處降者於邊五郡故塞外，而皆在河南，因其故俗為屬國。
sentences.literal: He then apportioned the surrendered people beyond the old barrier of five border commanderies, all south of the river, and according to their former customs made them dependent states.
sentences.idiomatic: The capitulators were resettled south of the river beyond the old wall of five frontier commanderies and organized as dependent states under their own customs.

sentences.chinese: 其明年，匈奴入右北平、定襄，殺略漢千餘人。
sentences.literal: The next year the Xiongnu entered Youbeiping and Dingxiang and killed or carried off more than a thousand Han.
sentences.idiomatic: The following year Xiongnu raiders struck Youbeiping and Dingxiang, killing or capturing over a thousand Han subjects.

sentences.chinese: 其明年，上與諸將議曰：「翕侯趙信為單于畫計，常以為漢兵不能度幕輕留，今大發卒，其勢必得所欲。」
sentences.literal: The next year the emperor deliberated with the generals, saying, “Marquis of Xi Zhao Xin has drawn plans for the shanyu, constantly supposing Han troops cannot cross the desert in light strength and linger; now if we mobilize a great host, the situation is sure to achieve what we desire.”
sentences.idiomatic: The next spring the emperor told his generals, “Zhao Xin the Marquis of Xi has advised the shanyu that Han armies cannot cross the desert in light order and remain there. If we now raise a massive force, we shall finally get what we want.”

sentences.chinese: 是歲元狩四年也。
sentences.literal: That year was the fourth year of Yuanshou.
sentences.idiomatic: The year was Yuanshou 4 (119 BCE).

sentences.chinese: 春，上令大將軍青、票騎將軍去病各五萬騎，步兵轉者踵軍數十萬，而敢力戰深入之士皆屬去病。
sentences.literal: In spring the emperor ordered Great General Qing and Swift-cavalry General Qubing each to have fifty thousand horse, foot soldiers and transport following the army numbering several hundred thousand, and the men who dared to fight hard and go deep all were assigned to Qubing.
sentences.idiomatic: That spring he gave each of the great general and the swift-cavalry general fifty thousand horsemen, with hundreds of thousands of infantry and train troops in their wake, while every officer eager to strike deep was placed under Qubing.

sentences.chinese: 去病始為出定襄，當單于。
sentences.literal: Qubing at first was to march out of Dingxiang and confront the shanyu.
sentences.idiomatic: Qubing was first slated to issue from Dingxiang and take the shanyu head-on.

sentences.chinese: 捕虜，虜言單于東，乃更令去病出代郡，令青出定襄。
sentences.literal: Captives said the shanyu was to the east, so he changed orders and had Qubing march out of Daicommandery and Qing out of Dingxiang.
sentences.idiomatic: Prisoners reported the shanyu lay to the east, so the plan was reversed: Qubing would sortie from Dai while Qing advanced from Dingxiang.

sentences.chinese: 郎中令李廣為前將軍，太僕公孫賀為左將軍，主爵趙食其為右將軍，平陽侯襄為後將軍，皆屬大將軍。
sentences.literal: Zhonglang commandant Li Guang was van general, Grand Coachman Gongsun He was left general, chief of the bureau of nobility Zhao Shiyi was right general, Marquis of Pingyang Xiang was rear general—all were subordinate to the great general.
sentences.idiomatic: Li Guang became van general, Gongsun He left general, Zhao Shiyi of the nobility bureau right general, and Xiang the Marquis of Pingyang rear general, all under the great general’s command.

sentences.chinese: 趙信為單于謀曰：「漢兵即度幕，人馬罷，匈奴可坐收虜耳。」
sentences.literal: Zhao Xin counseled the shanyu, saying, “When Han troops cross the desert, men and horses will be exhausted; the Xiongnu can sit and take them prisoner.”
sentences.idiomatic: Zhao Xin urged the shanyu: “Once the Han host crosses the desert, men and mounts will be spent—you can bag them at leisure.”

sentences.chinese: 乃悉遠北其輜重，皆以精兵待幕北。
sentences.literal: He then moved all baggage far to the north and with picked troops awaited them north of the desert.
sentences.idiomatic: Accordingly he shifted his baggage train far north and posted elite forces north of the sands to receive them.

sentences.chinese: 而適直青軍出塞千餘里，見單于兵陳而待，於是青令武剛車自環為營，而縱五千騎往當匈奴，匈奴亦從萬騎。
sentences.literal: It happened that Qing’s army emerged from the passes more than a thousand li and saw the shanyu’s troops drawn up waiting; Qing then ordered war wagons to ring themselves into a camp and loosed five thousand horse to engage the Xiongnu; the Xiongnu likewise sent ten thousand horse.
sentences.idiomatic: Qing’s column broke out of the barrier well over a thousand li and found the shanyu’s army formed and waiting. Qing had his armored wagons wheel into a fortified ring, then sent five thousand cavalry to probe the Xiongnu line; the enemy answered with ten thousand riders.

sentences.chinese: 會日且入，而大風起，沙礫擊面，兩軍不相見，漢益縱左右翼繞單于。
sentences.literal: As the sun was about to set a great wind arose, sand and gravel struck the face, the two armies could not see each other, and Han further loosed left and right wings to circle the shanyu.
sentences.idiomatic: Dusk was falling when a gale sprang up, sand blasting their faces until neither side could see. Han then threw both wings wide in an attempt to envelop the shanyu.

sentences.chinese: 單于視漢兵多，而士馬尚彊，戰而匈奴不利，薄莫，單于遂乘六臝，壯騎可數百，直冒漢圍西北馳去。
sentences.literal: The shanyu saw Han troops were many and men and mounts still strong; fighting, the Xiongnu fared ill; toward dusk the shanyu then mounted six mules, with stalwart horse of several hundred, and went straight through Han’s cordon northwest and fled.
sentences.idiomatic: The shanyu saw how numerous the Han were and how fresh their mounts; the Xiongnu line wavered. Near nightfall he sprang onto a team of six mules, surrounded by a few hundred picked horsemen, and bored straight through the Han ring toward the northwest.

sentences.chinese: 昏，漢匈奴相紛挐，殺傷大當。
sentences.literal: At dusk Han and Xiongnu grappled in confusion; kills and wounds were roughly matched.
sentences.idiomatic: In the twilight the two hosts tangled in a confused melee with roughly equal losses.

sentences.chinese: 漢軍左校捕虜，言單于未昏而去，漢軍因發輕騎夜追之，青因隨其後。
sentences.literal: Han’s left command captured prisoners who said the shanyu had left before dark; Han therefore sent light cavalry in pursuit by night, and Qing followed behind.
sentences.idiomatic: Prisoners taken by Han’s left wing reported that the shanyu had quit the field before nightfall; light horse were sent in night pursuit, with Qing trailing the pursuit column.

sentences.chinese: 匈奴兵亦散走。
sentences.literal: Xiongnu troops also scattered and fled.
sentences.idiomatic: The Xiongnu ranks likewise broke and ran.

sentences.chinese: 會明，行二百餘里，不得單于，頗捕斬首虜萬餘級，遂至窴顏山趙信城，得匈奴積粟食軍。
sentences.literal: By dawn they had gone more than two hundred li, did not catch the shanyu, but took or beheaded over ten thousand grades, then reached Mount Xianyan’s Zhaoxin City, got the Xiongnu’s stored grain, and fed the army.
sentences.idiomatic: At daybreak, after more than two hundred li without overtaking the shanyu, they had still chalked up over ten thousand heads. They pushed on to Zhaoxin City below Mount Xianyan, seized the enemy granaries, and fed the troops from them.

sentences.chinese: 軍留一日而還，悉燒其城餘粟以歸。
sentences.literal: The army halted one day and returned, burning all the city’s surplus grain before withdrawing.
sentences.idiomatic: They rested a day, torched the remaining stores in the city, and marched back.

sentences.chinese: 青之與單于會也，而前將軍廣、右將軍食其軍別從東道，或失道。
sentences.literal: When Qing met the shanyu, van general Guang and right general Shiyi’s army separately followed the eastern road and lost the way.
sentences.idiomatic: While Qing engaged the shanyu, van general Li Guang and right general Zhao Shiyi had taken a separate eastern track and gone astray.

sentences.chinese: 大將軍引還，過幕南，乃相逢。
sentences.literal: The great general led the return, crossed south of the desert, and then they met.
sentences.idiomatic: Qing brought his command back across the desert’s southern rim and there linked up with them.

sentences.chinese: 青欲使使歸報，令長史簿責廣，廣自殺。
sentences.literal: Qing wished to send messengers back to report and ordered the chief clerk to call Guang to account in the registers; Guang killed himself.
sentences.idiomatic: Qing meant to dispatch a courier to court and told his chief clerk to summon Li Guang for a formal inquiry; Guang took his own life.

sentences.chinese: 食其贖為庶人。
sentences.literal: Shiyi ransomed his life and became a commoner.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhao Shiyi commuted sentence by ransom and was reduced to a commoner.

sentences.chinese: 青軍入塞，凡斬首虜萬九千級。
sentences.literal: Qing’s army entered the passes; in all they beheaded or captured 19,000 grades.
sentences.idiomatic: When Qing’s army re-entered the barrier, its tally stood at nineteen thousand heads and prisoners.

sentences.chinese: 是時匈奴眾失單于十餘日，右谷蠡王自立為單于。
sentences.literal: At that time the Xiongnu host had lost the shanyu for more than ten days, and the right Guli king set himself up as shanyu.
sentences.idiomatic: For ten days and more the Xiongnu had been without their shanyu; the right Guli king declared himself ruler.

sentences.chinese: 單于後得其眾，右王乃去單于之號。
sentences.literal: Later the shanyu regathered his host, and the right king then dropped the title of shanyu.
sentences.idiomatic: When the true shanyu reassembled his following, the right king quietly abandoned his claim.

sentences.chinese: 去病騎兵車重與大將軍軍等，而亡裨將。
sentences.literal: Qubing’s cavalry, chariots, and baggage were equal to the great general’s army, but he had no lieutenant general.
sentences.idiomatic: Qubing fielded cavalry, wagons, and trains on the same scale as the great general yet commanded no lieutenant generals.

sentences.chinese: 悉以李敢等為大校，當裨將，出代、右北平二千餘里，直左方兵，所斬捕功已多於青。
sentences.literal: He wholly used Li Gan and others as grand colonels in the place of lieutenant generals, marched out of Dai and Youbeiping more than two thousand li, struck the left-wing host, and the heads he took already exceeded Qing’s.
sentences.idiomatic: He relied on Li Gan and others as grand colonels acting as lieutenant generals, drove more than two thousand li from Dai and Youbeiping into the eastern wing, and ran up a butcher’s bill that surpassed Qing’s.

sentences.chinese: 既皆還，上曰：「票騎將軍去病率師躬將所獲葷允之士，約輕齎，絕大幕，涉獲單于章渠，以誅北車耆，轉擊左大將雙，獲旗鼓，歷度難侯，濟弓盧，獲屯頭王、韓王等三人，將軍、相國、當戶、都尉八十三人，封狼居胥山，禪於姑衍，登臨翰海，執訊獲醜七萬有四百四十三級，師率減什二，取食於敵，卓行殊遠而糧不絕。
sentences.literal: When both had returned, the emperor said, “Swift-cavalry general Qubing led the army, personally commanding warriors of the Xunyu taken captive, traveling light across the great desert, crossed and seized the shanyu’s Zhangqu, executed Beicheqi, turned to strike the left grand general Shuang, captured drums and banners, passed Marquis Dunan, forded Gonglu, captured kings Tuntou and Han among three, eighty-three generals, chancellors, household heads, and commandants, sacrificed on Mount Langjuxu, performed the secondary rite at Guyan, climbed to overlook the Hanhai, took prisoners numbering 70,443 grades, the army lost two tenths of its strength, took food from the enemy, marched extraordinarily far yet never ran short of grain.
sentences.idiomatic: When both columns were home, the emperor announced: “The swift-cavalry general led Xunyu tribesmen he had impressed into his ranks, marched light across the desert, crossed the shanyu’s channel warden Zhangqu, slew King Beicheqi, turned on the left grand general Shuang, seized his drums and banners, passed the fief of Marquis Dunan, forded the Gonglu River, captured three kings including Tuntou and Han, together with eighty-three generals, chancellors, household heads, and commandants, sacrificed on Mount Langjuxu, performed the subsidiary rite at Guyan, looked out over the Han sea, took seventy thousand four hundred forty-three living captives, lost only two men in ten, fed his army from the enemy, and pushed farther than any prior column without running out of grain.”

sentences.chinese: 以五千八百戶益封票騎將軍。
sentences.literal: Increase the swift-cavalry general’s fief by 5,800 households.
sentences.idiomatic: Add fifty-eight hundred households to the swift-cavalry general’s fief.

sentences.chinese: 右北平太守路博德屬票騎將軍，會興城，不失期，從至檮余山，斬首捕虜二千八百級，封博德為邳離侯。
sentences.literal: Prefect of Youbeiping Lu Bode was subordinate to the swift-cavalry general, met at Xingcheng without missing the deadline, followed to Mount Chiyu, beheaded or captured 2,800—enfeoff Bode as marquis of Pilü.
sentences.idiomatic: Lu Bode, prefect of Youbeiping, served under the swift-cavalry general, kept his rendezvous at Xingcheng, followed to Mount Chiyu, and accounted for twenty-eight hundred heads—invest Lu Bode as marquis of Pilü.

sentences.chinese: 北地都尉衛山從票騎將軍獲王，封山為義陽侯。
sentences.literal: Commandant of Beidi Wei Shan followed the swift-cavalry general and captured a king—enfeoff Shan as marquis of Yiyang.
sentences.idiomatic: Wei Shan, commandant of Beidi, rode with the swift-cavalry general and seized a king—invest him as marquis of Yiyang.

sentences.chinese: 故歸義侯因淳王復陸支、樓剸王伊即靬皆從票騎將軍有功，封復陸支為杜侯，伊即靬為眾利侯。
sentences.literal: Former marquis of submission King Fuzhizhi of the Yichun and King Yijian of the Loujian both followed the swift-cavalry general with merit—enfeoff Fuzhizhi as marquis of Du and Yijian as marquis of Zhongli.
sentences.idiomatic: The former submission marquises—Fuzhizhi of the Yichun and Yijian of the Loujian—both distinguished themselves under the swift-cavalry general; Fuzhizhi became marquis of Du, Yijian marquis of Zhongli.

sentences.chinese: 從票侯破奴、昌武侯安稽從票騎有功，益封各三百戶。
sentences.literal: Marquis attendant on the ticket Ponu and Marquis of Changwu Anji followed the swift-cavalry with merit—increase each’s fief by 300 households.
sentences.idiomatic: Ponu the marquis attendant on the ticket and Anji the Marquis of Changwu both earned merit with the swift-cavalry general; each received three hundred added households.

sentences.chinese: 漁陽太守解、校尉敢皆獲鼓旗，賜爵關內侯，解食邑三百戶，敢二百戶。
sentences.literal: Prefect of Yuyang Xie and Colonel Gan both captured drums and banners and were granted rank as marquis within the passes; Xie received three hundred households of revenue, Gan two hundred.
sentences.idiomatic: The Yuyang prefect surnamed Xie and Colonel Li Gan each took enemy drums and standards and were made marquises within the passes—three hundred households for Xie, two hundred for Gan.

sentences.chinese: 校尉自為爵左庶長。」
sentences.literal: Colonel Ziwei was granted rank as left chief of the people.”
sentences.idiomatic: Colonel Ziwei was raised to left chief of the people.”

sentences.chinese: 軍吏卒為官，賞賜甚多。
sentences.literal: Officers and soldiers became officials; rewards and gifts were very many.
sentences.idiomatic: Officers and men were promoted and heaped with rewards.

sentences.chinese: 而青不得益封，吏卒無封者。
sentences.literal: But Qing did not receive an increased fief, and no officers or soldiers under him were enfeoffed.
sentences.idiomatic: Qing himself received no addition to his fief, and none of his subordinates were given new marquisates.

sentences.chinese: 唯西河太守常惠、雲中太守遂成受賞，遂成秩諸侯相，賜食邑二百戶，黃金百斤，惠爵關內侯。
sentences.literal: Only West River prefect Chang Hui and Yunzhong prefect Suicheng received rewards; Suicheng’s salary was raised to that of a feudal chancellor, he was granted two hundred households of revenue and a hundred pounds of gold, and Hui was given rank as marquis within the passes.
sentences.idiomatic: Only Chang Hui of West River and Suicheng of Yunzhong were singled out—Suicheng’s stipend was lifted to a feudal chancellor’s level, with two hundred households and a hundred pounds of gold, while Hui received the rank of marquis within the passes.

sentences.chinese: 兩軍之出塞，塞閱官及私馬凡十四萬匹，而後入塞者不滿三萬匹。
sentences.literal: Of the two armies’ crossing of the barrier, government and private horses inspected at the passes totaled 140,000, but those that re-entered the passes afterward were fewer than 30,000.
sentences.idiomatic: The two hosts had filed out with a hundred forty thousand government and private mounts past the barrier muster; fewer than thirty thousand animals came back in.

sentences.chinese: 乃置大司馬位，大將軍、票騎將軍皆為大司馬。
sentences.literal: He then established the office of grand marshal; the great general and swift-cavalry general both became grand marshals.
sentences.idiomatic: The court then created the post of grand marshal, and both the great general and the swift-cavalry general were named to it.

sentences.chinese: 定令，令票騎將軍秩祿與大將軍等。
sentences.literal: He fixed the statutes so that the swift-cavalry general’s rank and salary equaled the great general’s.
sentences.idiomatic: Regulations were revised so that the swift-cavalry general’s grade and pay matched the great general’s.

sentences.chinese: 自是後，青日衰而去病日益貴。
sentences.literal: From this time Qing daily declined while Qubing daily grew more honored.
sentences.idiomatic: Afterward Qing’s star waned as Qubing’s rose.

sentences.chinese: 青故人門下多去事去病，輒得官爵，唯獨任安不肯去。
sentences.literal: Many of Qing’s old friends and retainers went to serve Qubing and promptly received office and rank; only Ren An refused to go.
sentences.idiomatic: Former clients drifted from Qing’s gate to Qubing’s and walked away with titles; only Ren An stayed loyal.

sentences.chinese: 去病為人少言不泄，有氣敢往。
sentences.literal: Qubing as a man spoke little and did not leak secrets; he had spirit and dared to go forward.
sentences.idiomatic: Qubing was reticent and close-mouthed, but bold and decisive once in the field.

sentences.chinese: 上嘗欲教之吳孫兵法，對曰：「顧方略何如耳，不至學古兵法。」
sentences.literal: The emperor once wished to teach him the Wu and Sunzi arts of war; he replied, “It depends on strategy—there is no need to study ancient military texts.”
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor once offered to tutor him in the Wu and Sunzi classics; he answered, “Victory turns on planning, not on poring over old manuals.”

sentences.chinese: 上為治第，令視之，對曰：「匈奴不滅，無以家為也。」
sentences.literal: The emperor built him a mansion and bade him inspect it; he replied, “The Xiongnu are not yet destroyed—there is no time to think of a household.”
sentences.idiomatic: When the emperor had a fine house built for him and asked his opinion, he said, “Until the Xiongnu are gone, I have no business tending a private home.”

sentences.chinese: 由此上益重愛之。
sentences.literal: From this the emperor increasingly esteemed and loved him.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor only prized him the more for it.

sentences.chinese: 然少而侍中，貴不省士。
sentences.literal: Yet from youth he was a palace attendant, and in his eminence he did not pity the common soldier.
sentences.idiomatic: Raised young inside the palace, he never learned sympathy for the rank and file.

sentences.chinese: 其從軍，上為遣太官齎數十乘，既還，重車餘棄粱肉，而士有飢者。
sentences.literal: When he campaigned the emperor sent the grand provisioner with several tens of wagons; when he returned, surplus grain carts threw away good grain and meat while soldiers went hungry.
sentences.idiomatic: On campaign the throne sent the imperial kitchen after him with dozens of supply carts; on his return he left wagons full of spoiled grain and meat while his men starved.

sentences.chinese: 其在塞外，卒乏糧，或不能自振，而去病尚穿域鸲鞠也。
sentences.literal: When he was beyond the barrier, the troops lacked grain and some could not pull themselves up, yet Qubing still marked out a pitch and played cuju.
sentences.idiomatic: Beyond the wall, with men too weak from hunger to stand, he would still stake out a field and kick a ball.

sentences.chinese: 事多此類。
sentences.literal: Matters were mostly of this sort.
sentences.idiomatic: His conduct was often of that kind.

sentences.chinese: 青仁，喜士退讓，以和柔自媚於上，然於天下未有稱也。
sentences.literal: Qing was humane, delighted in gentlemen and yielding, and by mildness flattered the sovereign, yet in the realm there was none who praised him.
sentences.idiomatic: Qing was kindly, cultivated scholars, and deferred to others, winning favor through gentleness—yet the world offered him little praise.

sentences.chinese: 自去病死後，青長子宜春侯伉坐法失侯。
sentences.literal: After Qubing died, Qing’s eldest son Kang the marquis of Yichun lost his marquisate for violating the law.
sentences.idiomatic: After Qubing’s death, Qing’s eldest son Kang, marquis of Yichun, forfeited his title for a legal offense.

sentences.chinese: 後五歲，伉弟二人，陰安侯不疑、發干侯登，皆坐酎伉失侯。
sentences.literal: Five years later Kang’s two younger brothers—Buyi marquis of Yin’an and Deng marquis of Fagan—both lost their marquisates sitting for the libation gold offense implicating Kang.
sentences.idiomatic: Five years later Kang’s younger brothers—Buyi, marquis of Yin’an, and Deng, marquis of Fagan—also forfeited their titles when the communal-wine-gold inquest implicated Kang’s line.

sentences.chinese: 後二歲，冠軍侯國絕。
sentences.literal: Two years later the Champion marquisate line was cut off.
sentences.idiomatic: Two years after that the Champion marquisate died out for lack of an heir.

sentences.chinese: 後四年，元封五年，青薨，諡曰烈侯。
sentences.literal: Four years later, in the fifth year of Yuanfeng, Qing died and was posthumously titled marquis Lie.
sentences.idiomatic: Four years later, in Yuanfeng 5 (106 BCE), Qing died and received the posthumous title Marquis Lie.

sentences.chinese: 子伉嗣，六年坐法免。
sentences.literal: His son Kang succeeded; in the sixth year he lost office for violating the law.
sentences.idiomatic: His son Kang inherited the title but was stripped in the sixth year for a legal infraction.

sentences.chinese: 自青圍單于後十四歲而卒，竟不復擊匈奴者，以漢馬少，又方南誅兩越，東伐朝鮮，擊羌、西南夷，以故久不伐胡。
sentences.literal: From Qing’s encirclement of the shanyu to his death was fourteen years; in the end Han did not again strike the Xiongnu because Han horses were few, and moreover it was then executing the two Yue in the south, attacking Joseon in the east, and striking the Qiang and southwestern Yi—therefore for long it did not attack the Hu.
sentences.idiomatic: Fourteen years passed between Qing’s envelopment of the shanyu and his death; Han launched no further major strikes because horse stocks had dwindled, while campaigns consumed the south against both Yue, the east against Joseon, and the interior against the Qiang and southwestern tribes—so the northern frontier stayed quiet for years.

sentences.chinese: 初，青既尊貴，而平陽侯曹壽有惡疾就國，長公主問：「列侯誰賢者？」
sentences.literal: At first, when Qing had already become honored, Marquis of Pingyang Cao Shou had a vile illness and went to his fief; the Grand Princess asked, “Among the marquises who is worthy?”
sentences.idiomatic: Early in Qing’s eminence the Marquis of Pingyang, Cao Shou, fell gravely ill and retired to his estate. The Grand Princess asked her attendants, “Which of the marquises is worth taking?”

sentences.chinese: 左右皆言大將軍。
sentences.literal: Those at her side all said the great general.
sentences.idiomatic: Every voice named the great general.

sentences.chinese: 主笑曰：「此出吾家，常騎從我，柰何？」
sentences.literal: The princess laughed and said, “This one came out of my household and used to ride in attendance on me—how can that be?”
sentences.idiomatic: She laughed and said, “He was raised in my own house and used to ride at my stirrup—how could I marry him?”

sentences.chinese: 左右曰：「於今尊貴無比。」
sentences.literal: Those at her side said, “Today none is more honored.”
sentences.idiomatic: They answered, “No man in the realm now ranks higher.”

sentences.chinese: 於是長公主風白皇后，皇后言之，上乃詔青尚平陽主，與主合葬，起冢象廬山云。
sentences.literal: Thereupon the Grand Princess hinted to the empress, the empress spoke to the emperor, and the emperor then ordered Qing to marry the Princess of Pingyang; they were buried together, and a tumulus was raised modeled on Mount Lu.
sentences.idiomatic: She dropped a hint to the empress, who spoke to the throne; an edict then joined Qing in marriage to the Princess of Pingyang. They were laid in a shared tomb mound shaped to echo Mount Lu.

sentences.chinese: 最大將軍青凡七出擊匈奴，斬捕首虜五萬餘級。
sentences.literal: In sum Great General Qing seven times went out to strike the Xiongnu and beheaded or captured more than fifty thousand grades.
sentences.idiomatic: Great General Qing campaigned against the Xiongnu seven times and accounted for more than fifty thousand heads and prisoners.

sentences.chinese: 一與單于戰，收河南地，置朔方郡。
sentences.literal: Once he fought the shanyu, recovered the land south of the river, and established Shuofang commandery.
sentences.idiomatic: He met the shanyu in battle once, reclaimed the Ordos south of the Yellow River, and founded Shuofang commandery.

sentences.chinese: 再益封，凡萬六千三百戶；
sentences.literal: Twice he received additions to his fief, in all 16,300 households;
sentences.idiomatic: His fief was enlarged twice, for a total of sixteen thousand three hundred households;

sentences.chinese: 封三子為侯，侯千三百戶，并之二萬二百戶。
sentences.literal: he enfeoffed three sons as marquises, each marquisate 1,300 households, together with the previous making 20,200 households.
sentences.idiomatic: three sons were enfeoffed at thirteen hundred households each, bringing the combined household count to twenty thousand two hundred.

sentences.chinese: 其裨將及校尉侯者九人，為特將者十五人，李廣、張騫、公孫賀、李蔡、曹襄、韓說、蘇建皆自有傳。
sentences.literal: His lieutenant generals and colonels who were enfeoffed were nine; those who served as independent generals were fifteen; Li Guang, Zhang Qian, Gongsun He, Li Cai, Cao Xiang, Han Shuo, and Su Jian each have their own biography.
sentences.idiomatic: Nine lieutenant generals and colonels under him won marquisates; fifteen rose to independent command. Li Guang, Zhang Qian, Gongsun He, Li Cai, Cao Xiang, Han Shuo, and Su Jian are treated in separate chapters.

sentences.chinese: 李息，郁郅人也，事景帝。
sentences.literal: Li Xi was a man of Yuzhi and served Emperor Jing.
sentences.idiomatic: Li Xi came from Yuzhi and had served Emperor Jing.

sentences.chinese: 至武帝立八歲，為材官將軍，軍馬邑；
sentences.literal: When eight years had passed since Emperor Wu’s accession, he became timber-and-barracks general and encamped at Mayi;
sentences.idiomatic: Eight years into Emperor Wu’s reign he was made timber-and-barracks general and stationed at Mayi;

sentences.chinese: 後六歲，為將軍，出代；
sentences.literal: six years later he was general and marched out of Dai;
sentences.idiomatic: six years after that he held a general’s commission and advanced from Dai;

sentences.chinese: 後三歲，為將軍，從大將軍出朔方：皆無功。
sentences.literal: three years later he was general and followed the great general out of Shuofang: all without merit.
sentences.idiomatic: three years later he was again a general and followed the great general from Shuofang—each time without distinction.

sentences.chinese: 凡三為將軍，其後常為大行。
sentences.literal: In all he thrice was general; afterward he constantly was grand messenger.
sentences.idiomatic: He served three tours as a general, then spent the remainder of his career as grand messenger.
